page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24 is a memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 24 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 186.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core. It operates on the x86_64 architecture and includes a 260 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the c4-highmem-24 demonstrates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1913.0 and strong results in image and text processing. Its multi-core capabilities, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores, fall within the average tier. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a read-only cached small block bandwidth of 1671.9 GB/sec, while large block uncached read bandwidth reaches 139.8 GB/sec. Notably, the server achieves top-tier performance in LLM text generation for small models, reaching 394.04 tokens/sec, though medium and large model inference speeds scale down to average levels.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a high-capacity memory profile relative to its compute core count, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. However, the absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, requiring external storage attachments and limiting its utility for heavy GPU-accelerated deep learning. The server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, text and image processing pipelines, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24 is an x86_64 architecture server optimized for memory-intensive workloads. Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor, it delivers 24 dedicated vCPUs across 12 physical cores, supported by 186.0 GB of RAM and a 260 MB L3 cache. The hardware features advanced instruction sets, including Intel AMX and AVX-512, which assist in cryptographic and vector operations. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, the instance exhibits strong single-core performance, particularly in text and image processing, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models. Its balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for database operations, memory-bound applications, and general-purpose workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without the overhead of higher compute tiers.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24 is an x86_64 server featuring 24 vCPUs, 186 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.

bullet_points: Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores.
Equipped with 186.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.75 GB of memory per core.
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Strong single-core performance in text processing, image processing, and small-model LLM text generation.
Configured without local storage or GPU accelerators.
Best suited for database operations, memory-intensive applications, and text processing workloads.
